payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Upon finishing your business in Alfield, you acquired your new cart, new horses, and found your way on the Amber Road northward for a number of days, making your way toward Zadash. Along the way, you had some curious naps, you found some cool little landmarks from a war long past, you were nearly robbed and instead shooed them off with cries of extreme syphilis, and then entered the gates of Zadash yourselves. Upon which, you briefly met a character named Ulysses, who offered, at a bit of cost, to show you around town for the remainder of the day. Unfortunately, the sun set within the next couple of hours, but you were led toward the Pentamarket, where you did a little bit of shopping, offloaded some things you'd acquired in your previous venture in the mines where you battled the gnolls, encountered a purveyor of various magical artifacts named Enchanter Pumat Sol, acquired some baked goods from the market, and then finished up by making your way to The Leaky Tap, where you had just begun to cross the threshold when we left off last week. With that, coming in out of the now-cold night air, mouths filled with various crumbs and residue of sweets having been consumed on the walk, you enter the darkened interior of The Leaky Tap. Now, The Leaky Tap itself is a simple, but very large tavern. You can see there's space here for at least a dozen tables, and chairs and stools crowded around. While it lacks decor, it seems to have charm, with a rather ratty-looking band in the corner that's playing mostly on-key. It appears that a number of people who have found their way-- this is the Eastern Outersteads, I believe-- have wandered in to spend whatever coppers they want to throw towards a drink to send their cares away for the evening, and/or argue with each other about how better to make the day before them. Upon stepping into the chamber, you can see there's already about 25 patrons scattered amongst the tables, and the evening appears to be slowly crawling to more of a roar. There's chatter and whispers, there's laughter and the clanks of metal and glass. You can see already there's one long bar on the far end of the room that is broken in the center for a number of entryway hatches, and two busied bartenders rushing back and forth. What do you guys want to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Roll a d20 real quick, just for that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not bad, I did okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The full schedule will be announced for us at C2E2 early next week. You can buy tickets at c2e2.com, and tomorrow is the deadline to order badges to receive them by mail, so any badge orders after that will have to be held at will call. Just a heads up! I think that's the last of our announcements. With that, let's jump in to tonight's episode of Critical Role! [click, TV static] [groovy Critical Role theme]

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back. (laughing)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back. Last we left off, the newly decided-upon title for this adventuring group was announced to Bryce, and as such you are now referred to as the Mighty Nein.

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: Nein!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The Mighty No, in Zemnian.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Big nope.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: With a dangerous-looking front of the snout, that as soon as it spins to you and he looks at you with this big, toothy grin, you can see scars in areas where it's been split in the past. This guy's seen some rough action in his past. But as soon as the large dragon mouth curls up into this smile, the golden yellow eyes peeking from behind these stark white scales, themselves look a bit dusty--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And also we should rent some rooms.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Jester, it looks like there's a game of cards going on over there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay, I will--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just tossing it out there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I will do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You don't have to take my--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Or you could eat first; we've been traveling--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I saw that you were eyeballing the room. Alles gut?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Everything is good?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's fine. Yeah. Seems like a room. People in it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is accurate.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Sir, can I bother you for a second?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At that point, and hand comes up and he goes, "Excuse me, just a moment." Fills up two tankards on the side, slides them down a bit, grabs a couple silver, pockets it, comes back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He's busy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Turns back to you, and maybe a good solid inch or two taller than you, a burly-looking dragonborn--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (whistles)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's not that tall. You're not Grog anymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Who's that?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The sun's been set for a couple hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Brilliant, thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That was very effective.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I normally find shouting people's names really loudly tends to bring them to your table.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right. I wonder where her office is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We could ask.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We could look. Have we had food and drink yet?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you guys have eaten. And now you're in the process-- you don't have to roleplay every meal. (various eating noises)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nott has to roleplay every meal.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What time of the evening do I know it to be?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point, it's probably pushing close to ten o'clock.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ja, okay.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "There is. There is a Platinum House. That would also be in the Interstead Sprawl. Look for the beautiful white building with deep blue tapestries on the outside. They're reflective, mirror-like, during the day. It's almost a beacon for having to shield your eyes. Meditation, worship, is all available within there. Sermon's usually around noon."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Like private school?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you're looking for a comparison here, it would be upper-end public school, if anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Charter school?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Magnet programs?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure, we'll put it at a charter school.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Lotteries are a bitch!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But controlled by the Soltryce Academy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All controlled by the Soltryce.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes. "The head tutor there is an overworked and relatively stressed-out individual."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Head tutor, you said?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes. Mikael Drom. Very nice fellow. Eager to help, but the system has been difficult for him."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is there a shrine to the Dragon, as well?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No. Looking around the room, most of the folks in here look to be salt-of-the-earth folk of the city. A few folks look to be a little more wealthy, lower-to-middle class if you were to give any sort of comparison there, and they all look like they're a bit riled up. The dwarf, who had eyed you up previously, his eyes have these heavy bags under them, he's got this scraggly unkempt brown beard, and very short, almost buzz-cut hair on top. He's glaring at the two of you. He steps away from the crowd and keeps a very close eye. The gentleman at the top, "First off, to our new conversationalists, I am Dolan Thrym. As many of you know her, I once ran a very successful physician practice here in the Interstead Sprawl, one that my partner was the head physician. Well, he was unfortunately brought to an unearned level of shame, and his practice ruined, as well as our industry, by the house of Sutan naught but a year ago. We went from a comfortable living helping people to a life of squalor and shame. I know we're not the only one, as now the son of the house of Sutan, Ren, sits at the top of that practice. Took all of our clients and squeezes them for all of their worth. And I, for one, am sick of it". Everybody else in the group goes (grunt).

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're a bit skeptical. We're looking for support, just like any other wayward souls in this crazy town.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We seek knowledge.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We want to be able to trust our Empire, but maybe we need some reassurance in one way or another.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion check with advantage with his help.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Persuasion with advantage? That's good! I got a natural 18 with a plus one, so 19. Just being real vague.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You seem to be on the level. We are a group called the Knights of Requital, and we are discussing methods of changing the current political unhappiness in the city of Zadash and beyond."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: See, we're not the only ones. We felt like something's been missing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Tearing at our heart. It's an ache.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well. I will continue with my talk, if you don't mind."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Please, yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Preach on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Does that name mean anything to Caleb?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I'm sick of it and we can't sit back and let nothing be done about it. Now, would you like to take the floor, Ulog?" The dwarf who was watching you guys takes his-- doesn't leave you for a moment before he steps over and the crowd separates and he steps up onto the small block which at his height puts him about head level with most of the crowd. "As many of you know, I was a woodworker for many years, carving wood, did good carpentry. My wife, Illiana, is a good wife. About six months ago, she was up late at night picking up food for the night for the two of us in the Pentamarket. She was in the wrong place at the wrong time. There were some street bandits outside the Tri-Spires. They stabbed someone who had run in, ran clean into my wife, just as the Crownsguard descended. She got looped in with them, thrown in jail. I went and fought. There wasn't even a bit of a trial. The High-Rictor sent her straight to the Umber Dungeon, sentenced her 20 years for this business. It's been six months now, I can't even see her. I'm sick of it, and I think it's time we did something." Everyone in the crowd goes (grunts).

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (grunts)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I'm sick of it and we can't sit back and let nothing be done about it. Now, would you like to take the floor, Ulog?" The dwarf who was watching you guys takes his-- doesn't leave you for a moment before he steps over and the crowd separates and he steps up onto the small block which at his height puts him about head level with most of the crowd. "As many of you know, I was a woodworker for many years, carving wood, did good carpentry. My wife, Illiana, is a good wife. About six months ago, she was up late at night picking up food for the night for the two of us in the Pentamarket. She was in the wrong place at the wrong time. There were some street bandits outside the Tri-Spires. They stabbed someone who had run in, ran clean into my wife, just as the Crownsguard descended. She got looped in with them, thrown in jail. I went and fought. There wasn't even a bit of a trial. The High-Rictor sent her straight to the Umber Dungeon, sentenced her 20 years for this business. It's been six months now, I can't even see her. I'm sick of it, and I think it's time we did something." Everyone in the crowd goes (grunts).

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (grunts)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Beau and Fjord, thank you for joining us tonight. It goes without saying, please, if you don't mind, we prefer to keep these conversations personal. For obvious reasons."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fjord.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well, we're scheduled to meet here in the next two days or so. We're gearing up for a couple of important events."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We understand. Thank you for sharing with us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I know I, for one, have had my discontent with the Empire, so it's good to hear we're not alone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course. That's requital. I appreciate your time. Rest well, we'll meet soon. Dismissed!" Everyone begins to scatter and make their way back up to the tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We stay down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I'm going to wait behind. Wait to talk to Dolan?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Really appreciate what you said.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, thank you. So much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah, of course. I understand that you aren't from around here, so don't feel that you have to take too much of this to heart. Best we can do to try and keep spirits up. I believe that we can find a way to exact some change real soon."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It struck a chord. It mirrors a lot of what we've seen since we've been here. Do you meet very often? I'd love to come back.

payload.raw_text: He goes, "You look over that rooftop there, and you can see that-- " and you see this slowly tapering tower that then curves off at the very top to be a very dome-like pinnacle. The stonework itself appears to be like a smooth concrete looking texture just from this distance, and the way the morning sun is hitting it, half of it is just bright and reflective and really intricate carving and various tiers along the towers. He goes, "That there's the Archive. That's what you're looking for. So just look at that tower and keep going."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: And it's to the east, you were saying?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: From where you are now, you guys are in the Eastern Outersteads, it would be west. And he goes, "Actually (ugh), hold on just a second, I'll get to your meal. Follow me." He comes out from behind the bar and slaps it up, and puts an arm around your shoulder and leads you right immediately outside to the front of the tavern, pushing you along.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Let me have a look at that." He takes them, pulls on the chains, drags for a minute and he inspects them over. "This some real nice craftsmanship, you don't see something this well made. So where did you get this?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe scrap metal is a good thing for this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I say so, these have all seen some use. Not all that great. Sorry."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But also! Also, we have some other things!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There were then chains, the manacles. Cart, we definitely drug here. Definitely around Fjord's neck.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're selling these things?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Are they in the bag?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have them!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, were they in the bag?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We put everything in the bag, then it would be good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You couldn't fit the manacles in the bag. The one that was around the neck of the manticore.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Then we fucking had them around our necks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you had them in the cart. So you bring them along with you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We knew we were going to the blacksmith.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well these here, these are some nice metal."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And they were magic!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "They were magic."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I show him the rune.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They had to be real good metal to be magic!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He glances past you. Well, the manticore head is still affixed to the cart, by the way. The halfling looks past and he goes, "I'll take it you mean that thing. It's quite scary, well done. Ah, looking at all the weapons. The wood's mostly rotted, unfortunately. Some of the metal can be melted down and reused, have to hammer out all of the imperfections, smelt it all. The manacles look very nice. Tell you what. Give me the lot and the manacles, I'll give you both these axes and be in excess of about 20 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You couldn't fit the manacles in the bag. The one that was around the neck of the manticore.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Then we fucking had them around our necks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you had them in the cart. So you bring them along with you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We knew we were going to the blacksmith.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well these here, these are some nice metal."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And they were magic!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "They were magic."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I show him the rune.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They had to be real good metal to be magic!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Let me have a look at that." He takes them, pulls on the chains, drags for a minute and he inspects them over. "This some real nice craftsmanship, you don't see something this well made. So where did you get this?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This giant lion thing with a woman's head had them around it and then we killed the lion thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We found it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Did you guys bring the cart with you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well, if we got there then yeah, probably.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He glances past you. Well, the manticore head is still affixed to the cart, by the way. The halfling looks past and he goes, "I'll take it you mean that thing. It's quite scary, well done. Ah, looking at all the weapons. The wood's mostly rotted, unfortunately. Give me the lot and the manacles, I'll give you both these axes and be in excess of about 20 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They had to be real good metal to be magic!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This giant lion thing with a woman's head had them around it and then we killed the lion thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We found it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Did you guys bring the cart with you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well, if we got there then yeah, probably.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Well, you can see where the Archive is but it's maybe another ten minute walk to get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You guys head on towards where now?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The bath!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're in the pentagram, penta--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pentamarket.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So we're close to the library? I thought it was in that area.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, the library is-- the Archive you're talking about. It's in the western side of the Pentamarket.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so we're closer now then we were. I don't see the entrance from where we are, or do we?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it's not one of the tallest towers in the city. In fact, the Tri-Spires, as it's called, has three massive towers of all different designs that lord over the rest of the city. They're the first things you saw when you crested the hill on the way into the city the night before. This one is still visible from your perspective, though. It's a little more squat and maybe four or five stories tall.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The whole kit and caboodle is visible from here?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: In the middle of her next line, "Two weeks to--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All right. Hey, Caleb!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Right this way!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She's really scary.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is that-- fuck. Was that all right? Fuck.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's just a volume thing sometimes. Okay let's go look--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (louder) He said enthusiasm!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh jeez!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Was that not...? Let's go this way.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Baby steps.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so where are you heading first?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The Archive.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The Archive, okay. On the way another town crier crosses by; another individual, same garb, a middle aged woman, hair pulled back into a very tight bun with the tufts of curled hair around the edges. Same tabard in the front and she's shouting out, "Riot in Rexxentrum squashed for peace! Radical groups brought to justice under the crown!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, that's some dirty fucking shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I follow behind her and yell  Town crier has toilet paper stuck to her shoe! (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Two weeks' time to the Harvest Close Festival! Victory Pit in the Pentamarket, entry begins in four days!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is that-- fuck. Was that all right? Fuck.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's just a volume thing sometimes. Okay let's go look--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (louder) He said enthusiasm!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh jeez!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Was that not...? Let's go this way.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Baby steps.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so where are you heading first?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The Archive.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The Archive, okay. On the way another town crier crosses by; another individual, same garb, a middle aged woman, hair pulled back into a very tight bun with the tufts of curled hair around the edges. Same tabard in the front and she's shouting out, "Riot in Rexxentrum squashed for peace! Radical groups brought to justice under the crown!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, that's some dirty fucking shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I follow behind her and yell  Town crier has toilet paper stuck to her shoe! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In the middle of her next line, "Two weeks to--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So you do know what toilet paper is.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: In the Victory Pit in the Pentamarket. Entry begins in four days.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, that's some dirty fucking shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I follow behind her and yell  Town crier has toilet paper stuck to her shoe! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In the middle of her next line, "Two weeks to--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So you do know what toilet paper is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Two weeks' time to the Harvest Close Festival! Victory Pit in the Pentamarket, entry begins in four days!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, what did she just say?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Harvest Festival?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Harvest Close Festival.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Harvest Close.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Harvest Close. It's the end of the harvest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's in two weeks?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In two weeks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: In a fortnight.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You are very aware of the Stormlord. You knew the Stormlord well. Since the Menagerie Coast largely relies on port trade, it is one of the most-worshipped gods in the Menagerie Coast for people who travel in and out of the ports there. Overseas, safe passage amongst bodies of water, contests of strength, proving worth through action. Lot of very interesting elements that the two of you would know of that following.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Well, I know I have to be careful about some certain things. I think I'll be fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nobody's chasing after you or giving you any heat, are they?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Insight check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Which one? I am lying!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Generally you can say, "Do I believe her?" and then I'll tell you to make an insight check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Do I believe her? I do! Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm not in trouble for anything. I think, if anything, it would be because of the god I've chosen to follow.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Which is whom?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Jester!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's the Stormlord. I don't know if you know of him.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do any of us know what the Stormlord is?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. For basic religion, you would, because you've been outside the Empire. Those of you who grew up in the Empire, you would vaguely have an idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What about a big reader?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You would, maybe. Make a religion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You've been in here, catching up and soaking for about a half an hour, 40 minutes, so you're getting pretty close to losing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he was all like, "I'm mysterious."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We met a guy named Shakäste, he was super cool. White afro, really awesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Have you heard of him?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Totally blind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Everywhere he went, he was like, (shushing). He was super badass.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He said "baby" a lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He called us "baby."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: He called you a baby a lot?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: But in a charming way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I was into it, I didn't feel demeaned by it. It makes me feel good, makes me feel safe.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I felt demeaned, but in a good way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm just saying, maybe we can--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Be like that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I get out and I start drying my hair and try to get pretty.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys start making your way out of the bath, gathering your clothing once more, as you head back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I gather my clothing away from Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is this one still in disguise? Nott, are you still in disguise?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How long has it been?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's an hour, that it takes.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Those of you heading to the King's Hall, you make your way back to the familiar outside, this large center for law, order, and judgement within the city of Zadash. As you approach the doorways, the guards pass, but keep an eye very closely as you enter. On the interior, you can see there are two round tables with curved benches that meet the sides of it, and there you can see some of the crownsguard in the process of talking to various civilians and taking notes. There's conversation about-- pardon me. So?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you need health potions, because you might die?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I think chances are very low.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you have one?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I have the one you secured for me, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Come on, Nott, it'll be fine. We'll see if we can meet anybody else who you could make feel better about who they are.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Go have fun. Maybe you get another button or two from a grumpy person.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Excellent.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Before we leave, I run up and give Yasha a hug, and say: I'm glad you're back.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, thank you, Jester. I get uncomfortable. I don't hug her back, but I gently give her one pat.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Caleb sees this, and just goes--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hug around the arms that are down, and you're like--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Perfect.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm trying. I'm very uncomfortable with human touch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I feel like I know you better now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We'll see you back at the King's Hall.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okie-dokie.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Oh, let me go and get the Herald for you, hold on just a second." He scoots out of the next hallway. A few moments later, you watch as this really stressed-looking male dwarf with these thick mutton chops and salt-and-pepper hair, well-groomed, combed really heavily to one side, the thick hair is being tamed to the point where, in a very Gene Wilder way, it's combed and then (poof), out one side. As he walks in, he's wearing long robes of gold and white and silver, and carries the holy symbol of the Lawbearer. You can see an axe that hangs on a chain with a scale embedded in the center of it. He walks in. "Right. So you're inquiring about the job board?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is there a desk, or a clerk?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You don't see a desk or a clerk, but you do see the crownsguard that are currently at the tables, talking to these individuals, and you see two other individual crownsguards that are standing and guarding two hallway entrances that lead further into the King's Hall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: As we walk up, I'm going to take my robe, and for the first time any time this party has seen, cross it over and tie it more traditionally.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll walk up to one of the king's guard, ask him. We're here to inquire about one of the postings. There's a roving beast that needs taking care of.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "All right, Barry. Okay (clears throat). This here is a writ of Lawmaster intent. This will give you entrance to the location of where the job is supposed to take place. This supposed creature has holed up in a sewer nexus right on where the Interstead Sprawl meets with the outer wall of the Tri-Spires. We need you to travel down there, and, well, let's say there's a whole region on the inside where we've been hearing about this backing up of various liquid and strange noises from cleaners who were making their way through, so we sent a few crownsguards down and came across a mass of congealed sewage slime and webbing, so the fucking cowards ran back and we need to dig deeper. So here we are: find a way of whatever is creating this disturbance, kill it, take it back with proof of the kill, whatever built that little nest, and the Crown is willing to drop you about 700 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm sorry, but for the purposes of maintaining organization within the paperwork here we need to know the names of those who are to claim any rewards or benefits of completing a job."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm Philbin.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Philbin, all right. And you are?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Regis.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Damn it, I can't say it now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, fucker.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Lawrence.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Lawrence."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Kylre.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Kylre."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Esma.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Esma, all right."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Um. Barry. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Short for Barrymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's short for Barrymore, but I just go by Barry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The head-shake afterward is the best

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh! D&D Beyond! Am I remembering how to do this?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What screen are you on?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm still new.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Refreshing! What did you roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Refreshing, it's loading.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ashley, that's DocuSign, you're in the wrong app completely!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Really, oh wow, that's a number.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay, 13.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it a minus?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's a minus two.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "There have been three times that it's been taken up and one's come back declining it and two that have not."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Just to be clear, there's no clearage of sewage? We're just here to take care of whatever's causing that webbing you mentioned.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Whatever's causing the blockage, and the webbing, whatever else they found down there. Yes, take care of that, bring back proof of the death."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Proof of-- okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But you don't know what did it?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you know what kind of monster it is?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No fucking clue, that's why you're being paid 700 gold for it!"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Have people been killed?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "People have gone missing. You think you're the first to try and take the job? No, it's dangerous! That's why we're paying so much. If you don't want it, go away. If you want it, here's the writ, good luck."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Mighty obliged, we'll take care of this most expediently.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Bye.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Wait! Has anyone gone and come back?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: After asking some questions and making your way around you eventually find the Tri-Spires, which is not too difficult to see once you know what you're looking for. You see these three very unique-looking towers that ascend past a round wall that encases the interior of this elite portion of the city. As you approach the wall-- the outer walls you see are basic stonework and masonry, thick as they may be and used as from a defensive position-- these walls are made of solid marble, and look more decorative than designed to be a military buttress of any kind. As you approach, you can see there are two entrances to it-- or at least you've been told there are two. The one that's nearest to you opens in the direction of the Pentamarket, and while the portcullis is lifted you see there are two crownsguard there, currently standing with spears and shields at the ready. A horse-drawn carriage clomps by you and makes its way inside. You guys approach and one of the guards steps forward. "Can I help you, please?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Beau does have a good point, though. I mean, if we get down there and it's some world-ending monster--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can just run back up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We found a manticore, who knows, they make nests.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There's a blacksmith who knows.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do you think the blacksmith cares what the Empire is doing? The blacksmith is just trying to survive.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think the blacksmith will get ten gold for telling them if they find out there was a manticore down there that--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: If the blacksmith knows.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a good plan B.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's an emergency plan. It's a good plan B, I'll grant you that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I head to the outside and wait out by the posting where we last saw Caleb and Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Karen and Rothschild.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Karen and Rothschild.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to juggle.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As she leaves from the front desk, you can see there are two crownsguard that are assigned to the sides that are keeping a close watch on you, but they're lackadaisy through their day. You get the sense they probably don't have to work too hard this side of the city. About five minutes pass.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you very much, kind sir! I walk grandiosely up to the woman. Hello. I have a package that's being delivered to here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well. What is the name receiving?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I shall inquire. I'll be back here in just a moment."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you very much.

payload.raw_text: He exits the building. Another five minutes pass, and eventually the woman comes back and she goes, "I'm sorry, we don't have a package for that name here. Is there any other it might be under?"

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: They're both watching you, but not intently like hawks, more just making sure you're not doing anything silly. You watch as a rather bounding obese merchant in his late 40s or so comes barreling down the stairway, sniffling hard and rubbing his nose. He's dressed really well-- maybe a size too small, trying to reclaim a presentation maybe of a younger self and he hasn't quite come to terms with the fact that he's hitting that age. As he walks past, he goes, "Ah. Good day."

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He exits the building. Another five minutes pass, and eventually the woman comes back and she goes, "I'm sorry, we don't have a package for that name here. Is there any other it might be under?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is anybody watching me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They're both watching you, but not intently like hawks, more just making sure you're not doing anything silly. You watch as a rather bounding obese merchant in his late 40s or so comes barreling down the stairway, sniffling hard and rubbing his nose. He's dressed really well-- maybe a size too small, trying to reclaim a presentation maybe of a younger self and he hasn't quite come to terms with the fact that he's hitting that age. As he walks past, he goes, "Ah. Good day."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Good day.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: She leaves again for another few minutes. Comes back after that, "I'm sorry, there's nothing under that name."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is anybody watching me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They're both watching you, but not intently like hawks, more just making sure you're not doing anything silly. You watch as a rather bounding obese merchant in his late 40s or so comes barreling down the stairway, sniffling hard and rubbing his nose. He's dressed really well-- maybe a size too small, trying to reclaim a presentation maybe of a younger self and he hasn't quite come to terms with the fact that he's hitting that age. As he walks past, he goes, "Ah. Good day."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Good day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He exits the building. Another five minutes pass, and eventually the woman comes back and she goes, "I'm sorry, we don't have a package for that name here. Is there any other it might be under?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe under Lavorre?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'll check."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well, depends on the size. We have our general suites, we have the lordly suites, and then we have the diplomatic invitational suites. Pricing begins at ten gold per room for the basic suites, 20 for the next tier up, and for the diplomatic ones, those run about 50 gold a night."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She comes back out. "I'm terribly sorry, we do not have a package for or under any of those names."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, that's really weird. Okay. Well, tell you what, how about, because it's probably going to be coming, you know, I'm here really early for it, you know-- so if it comes for me-- when it does-- could you leave word with the guard outside the Tri-Spire?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Which one?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The guard house, you know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh. All right, okay."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Then I can come and ask if it's been delivered and then I can come, because I don't want to have to walk all the way here, you know, if it's not here for me yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well. I'll make a note of it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Unless I should maybe stay here, waiting for it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "If you like, we could set you up with a room."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Yes, how much would that be?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He leans forward and the hand reaches out and takes your chin a little bit and holds it there. There in the dark space of the hood, you can see the familiar verdant irises looking back and the warmth fills you once more, the comfort that kept you comfortable so many years alone in that room. "I think the more deeds you do, the more lives you change, the more confident brats you break-- She'll find you."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Mini-fridge.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Wet bar.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right? Hmm. Traveler? Are you here? Shit. Okay. I don't know where I'm staying tonight, Traveler, I might be here, I might be with my friends, but-- Okay, I'll see you soon, okay! And I run back out of the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You turn and in the hallway to where you entered, you see a familiar cloak of deep green, hooded.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps) Hi!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The cloak nods down towards you and the voice slowly cascades out from underneath, "I'm so sorry she's not aware that you're here now, but you're with friends."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Do you think you could help me, though?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Child, I think I help you quite a bit already."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know. You do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "What do you need?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know how to let her know-- I don't know what to do.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: By now, I'd say it's probably closer to three in the afternoon--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hey-- oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! Hi-- oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're clean, we're relaxed, we're ready to go into a sewer.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fitting, absolutely fitting.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: How did-- didn't you just--?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: This is his normal look-- I think it's very nice, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I do, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I thought we took a-- Hey, I am not a person to judge. I know I don't look-- It's fine. Where are we going again?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I like you. Do you want to hang with us for a while?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, this day is great!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We'll take it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Well, let's go kill something, yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're going to the outer wall of the Tri-Spire.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?
